intitule: Lesson 14: Reported Commands and Requests - Formative Assessment

enonce.texte: Identify the correct reported speech structure for each direct command or request. Choose the correct option.

questions.texte: Direct: "Sit down," the teacher said to the students. Reported: The teacher ______ to sit down.

sousQuestions.texte: Identify the verb used for a command (tell or ask).

questions.texte: Direct: "Please help me with this exercise," John said to Mary. Reported: John ______ to help him with the exercise.

sousQuestions.texte: Identify the verb used for a polite request (tell or ask).

questions.texte: Direct: "Don't open the door," the guard said to us. Reported: The guard ______ not to open the door.

sousQuestions.texte: Transform the negative command "Don't speak" into reported speech.

intitule: Lesson 14: Transforming Direct Commands into Reported Speech

enonce.texte: Transform the following direct commands and requests into reported speech. Pay attention to the change in pronouns, verb tense (to + infinitive), and the reporting verb (told/asked).

questions.texte: "Finish your homework," the father said to his son.
questions.reponse: The father told his son to finish his homework.

questions.texte: "Please, don't make noise," the librarian said to the students.
questions.reponse: The librarian asked the students not to make noise.

questions.texte: "Call me tomorrow," she said to me.
questions.reponse: She told me to call her the next day / the following day.

enonce.texte:
Context: You are the class delegate. The Discipline Master (Mr. Ndoumbe) gave several commands and requests during the morning assembly. Later, the Form 1 students (who were absent) ask you: "What did Mr. Ndoumbe say?"

Report the following instructions to the younger students.

questions.texte: Mr. Ndoumbe said to all students: "Line up in silence!"
questions.reponse: He told us to line up in silence.

questions.texte: He said to the boys: "Don't push each other."
questions.reponse: He told the boys not to push each other.

questions.texte: He said to the class delegates: "Please, collect the notebooks before 10 a.m."
questions.reponse: He asked the class delegates to collect the notebooks before 10 a.m.

enonce.texte: Your mother gave you several instructions before leaving for work. Your younger brother came home late and asks you: "What did Mom say?" Report her commands and requests.

questions.texte: Mom said: "Wash the dishes immediately after eating."
questions.reponse: Mom told us to wash the dishes immediately after eating.

questions.texte: Mom said: "Please, don't forget to lock the door."
questions.reponse: Mom asked us not to forget to lock the door.

questions.texte: Mom said: "Feed the dog and then do your homework."
questions.reponse: Mom told us to feed the dog and then do our homework.

sousQuestions.texte: Explain why 'your' becomes 'our' in the reported version.
sousQuestions.reponse: Because the pronoun 'your' refers to the children (us), and in reported speech, we change the pronoun to match the perspective of the speaker (the child reporting to the brother).
